questions.question: In plain terms, what is a large language model and how does it generate text one token at a time?
Explain the difference between a model's context window and its parameters. Why does a longer context window cost more at inference time?
focus: context = input tokens held in attention
attention is quadratic in sequence length

questions.question: What is the role of temperature and top-p sampling when generating from an LLM? When would you turn temperature down to zero?
Walk me through what actually happens inside a single transformer attention layer when it processes a sequence.
Compare fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation, and prompt engineering. Given a customer-support bot that must answer from a changing knowledge base, which would you reach for and why?
What distinguishes an AI agent from a single LLM call? Describe the core loop an agent runs.
How does tool or function calling work under the hood, and how do you stop an agent from looping forever or calling tools it shouldn't?
What is ReAct prompting and why does interleaving reasoning with actions help an agent?
Design a multi-agent system to research a topic and produce a report. How would you split responsibilities and have the agents coordinate?
How would you evaluate whether an agent is actually performing well in production, beyond it 'looking right'?
Design the backend for a real-time voice assistant: browser microphone in, spoken AI reply out. What are the main components and where does latency come from?
How would you serve a large model to thousands of concurrent users cost-effectively? Talk about batching, caching, and scaling.
Design a RAG pipeline end to end, from raw documents to a grounded answer. Where do quality problems usually creep in?
How do you handle hallucinations and unsafe outputs in a user-facing LLM product?
Explain QLoRA. Why does it let you fine-tune a model on a single consumer GPU when full fine-tuning would not fit?
Tell me about an AI or ML project you're proud of. What was your specific contribution and what was the impact?
Describe a time a model or system you built failed in production or gave bad results. How did you find out and what did you do?
The field moves fast. How do you decide what new AI techniques are worth adopting versus hype you should ignore?
How do you explain a complex model decision or a technical trade-off to a non-technical stakeholder?
You disagree with a teammate on whether to use a third-party API or build a model in-house. How do you approach the disagreement and reach a decision?
